situation: audit-2026-07-03-012 SCRIPTS-L01 (priority 3, route-fix) pending. verify-pattern.sh heredoc block derived CAL/MD paths via f-string from P, contradicting GATE-0's sys.argv pattern and re-introducing shell-variable coupling inside a quoted PY block. Root /home/admin/workspace/scripts copy was partially fixed; autonomous-studio/scripts copy (the actual git-tracked file) still had both the $CAL interpolation in GATE-0 python -c string AND the f-string derivation in the heredoc.

Smoke-tested against autonomous-studio project — all 4 gates pass. audit_findings.finding: GATE-0 python -c interpolated $CAL directly into single-quoted string; paths containing apostrophes or shell metacharacters would break parsing or execute unintended code. Also inconsistent with the already-fixed /home/admin/workspace/scripts copy.
audit_findings.remediation: Use sys.argv to pass CAL path: python3 -c 'import json,sys; json.load(open(sys.argv[1]))' "$CAL"

audit_findings.finding: Python heredoc derived CAL and MD via f"{P}/..." from sys.argv[1] instead of receiving them as explicit args. Redundant derivation creates two sources of truth for the same path and defeats the injection-hardening goal of the quoted heredoc.
audit_findings.remediation: Pass CAL and MD as $5 and $6 on the python3 invocation; read via sys.argv[5], sys.argv[6] inside the heredoc.
